patterns.name: Bottega Â· Swipe Vertical PDP
patterns.tagline: Editorial product page that abandons the thumbnail grid for a full-bleed vertical image stack with a sticky purchase sheet.

tokens.grid: Single-column full-bleed image stack on mobile Â· two-up split block on desktop. No max-width on hero images.

patterns.name: Vertical Block Image Stack
patterns.purpose: Replace the carousel with sequenced full-bleed images so the user scrolls through the product like an editorial spread.
patterns.evidence: DOM: `.c-pdp__topblockimages` + `.c-pdp__bottomblockimages__wrapper` â€” every image rendered as its own stacked block; mobile shows top block, desktop pairs into two-up via `c-pdp__blockimages--twoimages`.
patterns.where_to_use: High-consideration product pages where image quality is the proof. Avoid for SKU-heavy commodity catalogs.

patterns.name: Sticky Purchase Sheet
patterns.purpose: Decouple decision from discovery â€” CTA, size, and price stay anchored while content flows past.
patterns.where_to_use: Long-form PDPs or any flow where conversion shouldn't compete with scroll depth.

patterns.name: Discover-Hint Onboarding
patterns.purpose: Educate the visitor that the page rewards scrolling â€” luxury sites can't rely on user instinct.
patterns.where_to_use: Non-standard layouts that diverge from e-commerce convention.

patterns.name: BEM with Layout/Component/Utility Prefixes
patterns.purpose: Self-documenting class names that scope responsibility â€” `l-` for layout, `c-` for component, `u-` for utility, `@md` / `@lg` viewport modifiers (`u-hidden@md`).
patterns.where_to_use: Large product surfaces with many overlapping responsive variants.

patterns.name: Universal Lazyload
patterns.purpose: Every image deferred until viewport-near; first paint stays editorial-fast despite the heavy image stack.
patterns.evidence: Class `lazyload` applied to nav, PDP, carousel images alike â€” single shared loader.
patterns.where_to_use: Any image-heavy long page. Pair with LQIP placeholders for real polish.

patterns.notes: Counterintuitive move: Bottega trades the conventional thumbnail-strip carousel for a vertical editorial cadence. The page reads more like a magazine spread than a product configurator. Conversion is preserved by the sticky purchase sheet â€” so scroll-depth and add-to-cart remain decoupled. Worth stealing on any product surface where the imagery is the argument.
patterns.stack_hint: Salesforce Commerce Cloud (Demandware) â€” `Sites-BV-R-WEUR-Site` in CSS path; CDN `bottega-veneta.dam.kering.com` (Kering DAM).
